ReadRange.note: Self-closing tag. Classic Workbook activity — no Excel app needed.
ReadRange.description: Classic Workbook 'Read Range' — read a worksheet range into a DataTable without opening Excel.
ReadRange.use_when: User wants to read a sheet/range into a DataTable via the classic Workbook API (no ExcelApplicationScope). For modern Excel (X) projects use ReadRangeX; for classic-with-Excel use ExcelReadRange.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The workflow uses the modern Modern Excel (X) suite with Use Excel File scope rather than the classic ExcelApplicationScope.
You only need a single cell, not a rectangular range.

WriteRange.description: Classic Workbook 'Write Range' — write a DataTable into a worksheet range without opening Excel.
WriteRange.use_when: User wants to push a DataTable to a sheet via the classic Workbook API (no ExcelApplicationScope). For modern Excel (X) projects use WriteRangeX; to append rather than overwrite use AppendRange.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The workflow uses the modern Excel (X) suite with Use Excel File scope rather than classic Workbook scope.
You are writing a single value, not a DataTable's worth of rows.
The destination already has data and you want to append below it instead of overwriting from the start cell.

WriteCell.description: Classic Workbook 'Write Cell' — write a single value or formula into one cell without opening Excel.
WriteCell.use_when: User wants to set one cell via the classic Workbook API. For a whole DataTable use WriteRange; for modern Excel (X) projects use WriteCellX.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You are writing a DataTable or multiple cells, not a single cell.
The workflow uses the modern Excel (X) suite rather than classic Workbook activities.

AppendRange.description: Classic Workbook 'Append Range' — append a DataTable below the existing data in a sheet without opening Excel.
AppendRange.use_when: User wants to add rows under existing data via the classic Workbook API. To overwrite from row 1 use WriteRange; for modern Excel (X) projects use AppendRangeX.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The destination is empty (or you want to overwrite) rather than append below existing data.
The workflow uses the modern Excel (X) suite rather than classic Workbook activities.

ReadPDFText.description: Extract embedded text from a PDF file into a String variable.
ReadPDFText.use_when: User wants the text layer of a digitally-generated PDF. For scanned/image PDFs use ReadPDFWithOCR; for the page count use GetPDFPageCount.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The PDF is scanned or image-based and has no embedded text layer.

ReadPDFWithOCR.OCREngine.content: ActivityFunc x:TypeArguments='sd:Image, scg:IEnumerable(scg:KeyValuePair(sd1:Rectangle, x:String))' with DelegateInArgument and GoogleOCR (Tesseract) element

ReadPDFWithOCR.description: Run OCR over a PDF (page-by-page rasterisation) and return the recognised text.
ReadPDFWithOCR.use_when: User wants text from a scanned or image-only PDF. For PDFs with an embedded text layer use ReadPDFText (faster, lossless).

alternatives.use_instead_when: The PDF already has an embedded text layer; ReadPDFText is faster and lossless.

SendMail.ConnectionDetailsBackupSlot.content: BackupSlot x:TypeArguments='umae:ConnectionDetails' with IntegrationService and LegacyConfiguration lists

SendMail.description: Send an email through an SMTP server (or Integration Service connection).
SendMail.use_when: User wants to send mail via plain SMTP or an Integration Service mail connection. For Outlook desktop use SendOutlookMail; for Exchange Web Services use SendExchangeMail.

alternatives.use_instead_when: There is a logged-in Outlook desktop client and you want messages to be sent through it (so they land in Sent Items, follow Outlook rules, etc.).
You can authenticate to Exchange Web Services directly rather than relying on a generic SMTP relay.

GetIMAPMailMessages.description: Read email messages from an IMAP mailbox folder into a List<MailMessage>.
GetIMAPMailMessages.use_when: User wants to fetch mail from an IMAP server (folders, server-side flags). For POP3 use GetPOP3MailMessages; for Exchange/EWS use GetExchangeMailMessages; for an Outlook desktop client use GetOutlookMailMessages.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The mailbox only exposes POP3 (one-shot download) rather than IMAP (folders, server-side state).
You can talk to the Exchange Web Services / EWS endpoint directly with credentials rather than going through a generic IMAP gateway.
The robot has a logged-in Outlook desktop client and you want to read from the local profile rather than hit the server over IMAP.

SaveMailAttachments.description: Save the attachments of a MailMessage to a folder on disk, optionally filtered by extension.
SaveMailAttachments.use_when: User has a MailMessage from any Get*MailMessages activity and wants its attachments written to disk. Pair with GetIMAPMailMessages, GetOutlookMailMessages, or GetExchangeMailMessages.

DatabaseConnect.description: Open a database connection from a connection string and return a DatabaseConnection variable for reuse.
DatabaseConnect.use_when: User wants to share one connection across multiple subsequent ExecuteQuery / ExecuteNonQuery / BulkInsert / DatabaseTransaction calls. Always pair with DatabaseDisconnect; for one-off queries skip this and pass the connection string directly to ExecuteQuery.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You already have an open connection and want to close it instead of opening a new one.

ExecuteQuery.Parameters.content: scg:Dictionary x:TypeArguments='x:String, p:Argument' with InArgument elements (only when parameters provided)

ExecuteQuery.description: Run a SELECT query against a database and return the rows as a DataTable.
ExecuteQuery.use_when: User wants result rows from a SQL SELECT (or any query that returns a row set). For INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE without a result set use ExecuteNonQuery; for high-volume bulk loads use BulkInsert.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The statement is INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL and you only need the affected-row count, not a result set.
You are loading a large DataTable into a single table; BulkInsert is faster than parameterised inserts.

ExecuteNonQuery.Parameters.content: scg:Dictionary x:TypeArguments='x:String, p:Argument' with InArgument elements (only when parameters provided)

ExecuteNonQuery.description: Run a SQL statement that does not return rows (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL) and return the affected-row count.
ExecuteNonQuery.use_when: User wants to mutate the database (insert/update/delete) or run DDL. For SELECT use ExecuteQuery; for batched loads use BulkInsert; to wrap several statements atomically use DatabaseTransaction.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The statement is a SELECT and you need the result rows.
You are loading a large DataTable into a single target table.
Several statements must commit or roll back together.

BulkInsert.note: Requires prior DatabaseConnect whose DatabaseConnection output is passed as db_connection_variable. TableName is a literal column-matched table. DataTable must match the target schema. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
BulkInsert.description: Bulk-load every row of a DataTable into a target database table using the provider's bulk-copy API.
BulkInsert.use_when: User has a DataTable whose schema matches an existing table and needs the fastest insert path. Requires a prior DatabaseConnect. For row-by-row inserts use ExecuteNonQuery; to update existing rows use BulkUpdate; to insert one DataTable through the same code path use InsertDataTable.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The DataTable rows already exist and need to be updated by primary key rather than appended.
You want a generic DataTable insert without the provider's bulk-copy fast path.
Only a handful of rows; parameterised INSERTs are simpler than wiring DatabaseConnect+BulkInsert.

BulkUpdate.note: Requires prior DatabaseConnect whose DatabaseConnection output is passed as db_connection_variable. TableName is a literal column-matched table. DataTable must match the target schema. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
BulkUpdate.description: Bulk-update existing rows in a target database table from a DataTable matched on primary key.
BulkUpdate.use_when: User has a DataTable whose rows already exist in the target table and need their non-key columns refreshed. Requires a prior DatabaseConnect. To insert new rows use BulkInsert.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The rows are new and should be appended rather than matched and updated.

InsertDataTable.note: Requires prior DatabaseConnect whose DatabaseConnection output is passed as db_connection_variable. TableName is a literal column-matched table. DataTable must match the target schema. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
InsertDataTable.description: Insert every row of a DataTable into a target database table via parameterised INSERTs.
InsertDataTable.use_when: User wants to push a DataTable into a table without the provider-specific bulk-copy fast path. For high-volume loads use BulkInsert; for hand-written SQL use ExecuteNonQuery.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The DataTable is large and the provider supports a bulk-copy API.
You want explicit SQL/parameter control rather than the implicit column mapping.

db_connection_variable.note: DatabaseConnection output from the matching DatabaseConnect

DatabaseDisconnect.note: Self-closing. Pair with a preceding DatabaseConnect. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
DatabaseDisconnect.description: Close a previously opened DatabaseConnection and release its underlying resources.
DatabaseDisconnect.use_when: User wants to release a connection opened by DatabaseConnect (typically in a Finally block). To open the connection use DatabaseConnect.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You need to open a new connection rather than close an existing one.

DatabaseTransaction.note: Wraps statements in a transaction. Body must contain the DB activities to execute transactionally. Generator emits empty Sequence placeholder — caller must inject body activities. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
DatabaseTransaction.description: Scope that runs nested database activities inside a single transaction (commit on success, rollback on error).
DatabaseTransaction.use_when: User wants several ExecuteNonQuery / ExecuteQuery / BulkInsert calls to commit or roll back atomically. For one-off statements skip the transaction wrapper.

page_number_variable.note: Int32 variable or literal bracketed: e.g. [1]

ExportPDFPageAsImage.note: Namespace upap: = clr-namespace:UiPath.PDF.Activities.PDF;assembly=UiPath.PDF.Activities. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
ExportPDFPageAsImage.description: Render one page of a PDF to a PNG/JPG image file at the requested DPI.
ExportPDFPageAsImage.use_when: User wants a single PDF page as a raster image (for thumbnails, OCR pre-processing, embedding in reports). For all images embedded inside the PDF use ExtractImagesFromPDF; for OCR text use ReadPDFWithOCR.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You want the images that are already embedded in the PDF (not rasterised page snapshots).

ExtractImagesFromPDF.description: Save every embedded image in a PDF to files in an output folder.
ExtractImagesFromPDF.use_when: User wants the original images embedded inside a PDF (logos, photographs, charts). For rasterised page snapshots use ExportPDFPageAsImage.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You want a rasterised snapshot of an entire page rather than the embedded image objects.

ExtractPDFPageRange.description: Extract PDF Page Range activity from the integrations category.
ExtractPDFPageRange.use_when: User wants to integrate with an external system using Extract PDF Page Range.

GetPDFPageCount.description: Get PDF Page Count activity from the integrations category.
GetPDFPageCount.use_when: User wants to integrate with an external system using Get PDF Page Count.

JoinPDF.description: Join PDF activity from the integrations category.
JoinPDF.use_when: User wants to integrate with an external system using Join PDF.

password_variable.note: Current password (needed when opening a protected PDF)

owner_password_variable.note: New owner password (set to apply protection; empty to remove)

ManagePDFPassword.note: Password and OwnerPassword are surfaced in the Studio properties panel but omitted from the default XAML skeleton. Review required. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
ManagePDFPassword.description: Manage PDF Password activity from the integrations category.
ManagePDFPassword.use_when: User wants to integrate with an external system using Manage PDF Password.

ReadXPSText.note: XPS analog to ReadPDFText. Same namespace prefix (ui:). (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
ReadXPSText.description: Read XPS Text activity from the integrations category.
ReadXPSText.use_when: User wants to integrate with an external system using Read XPS Text.

ReadXPSWithOCR.note: ReadXPSWithOCR requires an OCREngine ActivityFunc delegate (same shape as ReadPDFWithOCR). Emit via hand-written generator (see gen_read_pdf_with_ocr in integrations.py) rather than data-driven fallback. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
ReadXPSWithOCR.description: Read XPS With OCR (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
ReadXPSWithOCR.use_when: Do not auto-generate Read XPS With OCR. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.
